html:
Exclusive to:

The Tar Lamp is a craftable Light Item in the Shipwrecked DLC. It requires a Seashell and some Tar to craft. It has the same burn time as a Torch (75 seconds), and is similarly affected by Strong Winds and Rain. It can be equipped to the hand slot, or placed on the ground or water similar to a Bottle Lantern, where it can be turned on or off. Tar Lamps can also be attached to Boats in the lantern slot.

Tar Lamps can be refueled to 100% with a single Tar . If the Tar Lamp is on the ground when it is depleted, it will drop ash.

Trivia

  • Tar Lamps were added to the Shipwrecked DLC more than a year after its official completion in the Home Sea Home update, after being tested in a beta branch for several months.
  • When right clicking on a Tar Lamp equipped to the hand slot, instead of being unequipped, it will be turned off, but remain equipped.

Gallery

  • Tar Lamp as seen in the poster for the Home Sea Home update.
Tools and WeaponsCraftable Items

The Tar Lamp is a craftable Light Item in the Shipwrecked DLC. It requires a Seashell and some Tar to craft. It has the same burn time as a Torch (75 seconds), and is similarly affected by Strong Winds and Rain. It can be equipped to the hand slot, or placed on the ground or water similar to a Bottle Lantern, where it can be turned on or off. Tar Lamps can also be attached to Boats in the lantern slot.
Tar Lamps can be refueled to 100% with a single Tar . If the Tar Lamp is on the ground when it is depleted, it will drop ash.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Tar Lamps were added to the Shipwrecked DLC more than a year after its official completion in the Home Sea Home update, after being tested in a beta branch for several months.
When right clicking on a Tar Lamp equipped to the hand slot, instead of being unequipped, it will be turned off, but remain equipped.

The '''Tar Lamp''' is a craftable [[Light]] [[Item]] in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC. It requires a [[Seashell]] and some [[Tar]] to craft. It has the same burn time as a [[Torch]] (75 seconds), and is similarly affected by [[Strong Winds]] and [[Rain]]. It can be equipped to the hand slot, or placed on the ground or water similar to a [[Bottle Lantern]], where it can be turned on or off. Tar Lamps can also be attached to [[Boats]] in the lantern slot.

Tar Lamps can be refueled to 100% with a single [[Tar]] . If the Tar Lamp is on the ground when it is depleted, it will drop ash.

=={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia==
* Tar Lamps were added to the ''Shipwrecked'' DLC more than a year after its official completion in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked#November 21st, 2017 - Home Sea Home Update|Home Sea Home]]'' update, after being tested in a beta branch for several months.
* When right clicking on a Tar Lamp equipped to the hand slot, instead of being unequipped, it will be turned off, but remain equipped.
